title: ballota hirsuta subsp. gracilis
intro: Ballota hirsuta subsp. gracilis, also known as Woolly Harebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and providing relief from digestive problems. Its medicinal properties can be utilized through herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
